data.body:
PASS 1 review — T11 (Data-migration / backfill safety) [overlay]: NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS.

This cluster is a byte-preserving DRY refactor: it consolidates canonical-JSON/content-hash onto the `_store/canonical.py` seam while explicitly "changing zero output bytes at any site." No schema, persisted format, or stored-hash value changes, and no data backfill is performed. Per T11's overlay scope (applies only when changing a persisted format or backfilling data) and its ANTI-FP note (not-applicable for non-persisted changes), none of the T11 sub-checks (expand-contract, batching, resumability, dual-write window, rollback/data-loss) are engaged.

Affirmation: the plan actively guards the persisted-bytes invariant rather than migrating it — e.g. `conflict_resolver._hash_value` keeps `ascii_only=True` to preserve the stored `value_hash`, `mutation.serialize_manifest` preserves `ensure_ascii=True`, and AC includes a characterization test asserting unchanged output bytes plus an unchanged sign→verify round-trip. No T11 findings.
